meaning: adj.被剥夺的,失去亲人的,丧失的
examples: The place seemed to be utterly bereft of human life.
For those of us in the latter group—consigned to coach, bereft of Flash Pass, too poor or proper to pay a placeholder—what do we do?
I grew up bereft of hugs.
Scottish Conservative communities and housing spokesman Alex Johnstone accused the government of being "bereft of ideas".
How and Bell, so bereft of form before the innings, slowly got into their stride.
